title: GitHub Copilot Across Environments
objectives: Compare Copilot across IDE, Chat, GitHub.com, and CLI
Master file references, slash commands, and agents
Understand CLI workflows and GitHub.com integration

sections.title: Copilot Integrations Across All Environments
sections.body: GitHub Copilot integrates across multiple development environments: IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains), GitHub.com web interface, command line, and the GitHub Copilot Chat interface. Each environment has specialized strengths for different workflow phases and team roles.

sections.title: Code Completion and Language Support
sections.body:
**Supported Languages:**
Python, JavaScript, Java, TypeScript, Ruby, Go, C#, C++ (with exceptional support), plus many others.

**Using Comments for Suggestions:**
Copilot leverages comments to understand intent and generate relevant suggestions.

**Comment Types Supported:**
- Inline comments — brief explanations beside code
- Block comments — detailed descriptions
- Docstrings — structured documentation (e.g., Python)
- TODO comments — notes for future work
- API documentation — method and parameter descriptions

**Comment-Driven Code Generation:**
Copilot uses comments to generate implementations, improve completions, suggest variable names, and select algorithms.

sections.title: Copilot Chat and GitHub.com Features
sections.body:
**When to Use Copilot Chat:**
- Complex code generation (algorithms, boilerplate, regex, SQL)
- Debugging assistance (errors, logical issues)
- Code explanations (complex code, patterns)
- Code refactoring (readability, performance)
- Test generation (unit tests)
- Documentation creation (docstrings, API docs)

**Scope Referencing:**
- File references: `#file:authController.js`
- Environment: `@terminal` for command-line context

**Slash Commands:**
- `/doc` — Generate documentation
- `/explain` — Explain code
- `/fix` — Suggest fixes
- `/generate` — Create new code
- `/optimize` — Improve performance
- `/tests` — Generate tests

**Agents:**
- `/new` — Create new projects
- `@terminal` — Command-line help
- `@vscode` — VS Code-specific help

sections.title: GitHub.com and CLI Workflows
sections.body:
**GitHub.com Features:**
- Pull request summaries
- Code review assistance
- Issue management
- Repository exploration
- Error explanation in GitHub Actions

**Copilot CLI:**
Install via Homebrew. Launch with `copilot`. Use interactive mode or one-shot prompts:
```bash
copilot -i "explain brew install git"
copilot -i "suggest find large files"
```

**CLI Slash Commands:**
- `/help` — Show available commands
- `/explain <command>` — Explain shell command
- `/suggest <task>` — Suggest command for task
- `/revise` — Revise last suggestion
- `/feedback` — Submit feedback
- `/exit` — Exit interactive mode

sections.title: Cross-Environment Practice: Inventory Sync Incident
sections.body:
### Real-World Use Case
Business Context:
A production issue shows differences between warehouse stock and app stock. The team must inspect code, PRs, logs, and scripts quickly to restore data consistency.

Developer Task:
Use Copilot across IDE, Chat, GitHub.com, and CLI to diagnose inventory sync failures, patch the sync job, and prepare safe deployment.

Example Input:
```json
{
  "itemId": "ITM-9001",
  "expectedQuantity": 540,
  "actualQuantity": 490,
  "syncJob": "nightly-inventory-sync",
  "lastRun": "2026-06-21T01:00:00Z"
}
```

Expected Output:
- Root-cause analysis identifying mapping or merge bug.
- Patch with updated tests and PR summary.
- CLI command plan for safe backfill and verification.

Why This Matters:
Wrong inventory counts lead to out-of-stock errors and delayed deliveries.

GitHub Copilot Challenge:
Use IDE for code fixes, Chat for explanation and tests, GitHub.com for PR review, and CLI for safe backfill commands.

exercise.title: Resolve inventory sync incident across Copilot environments
steps: Patch sync mapping logic with IDE suggestions.
Use Chat with #file references to explain defect and test strategy.
Use GitHub.com Copilot to review PR risks before merge.
Use CLI Copilot to generate backfill and verification commands.
Write a short incident summary and follow-up actions.
expectedOutcomes: Tested sync fix with safe deployment guidance.
Strong practical use of all Copilot environments.
troubleshooting: If CLI commands look risky, ask for dry-run alternatives first.
If PR review misses edge cases, ask explicitly for duplicate/merge checks.
If explanations are weak, include exact file and failing test context.
